scenes.text: In this scene, we will introduce a basic JavaScript function that demonstrates the creation and invocation of functions. Understanding functions is critical because they allow you to reuse code efficiently and organize your scripts better. We'll define a simple function that takes two numbers, adds them, and returns the result. This function illustrates fundamental programming constructs including parameters, return values, and the `console.log` method.

We will now visualize how conditional statements can guide the flow of logic in a program using a Mermaid flowchart. This scene will demonstrate a decision-making process, highlighting the use of 'if-else' constructs in programming. Such decisions allow programs to react differently based on conditions, thereby adding intelligence and flexibility to applications.
scenes.image_prompt:
flowchart TD
    Start[Start] --> Check_Condition{Is X > 10?}
    Check_Condition -->|Yes| Action1[Execute Task A]
    Check_Condition -->|No| Action2[Execute Task B]
    Action1 --> End[End]
    Action2 --> End
